questions.question: Why does OFDM perform better than single-carrier systems in frequency-selective channels?

answers.a: Each subcarrier experiences flat fading due to narrow bandwidth
answers.b: All subcarriers experience the same channel response
answers.c: OFDM avoids multipath fading using time-domain filtering
answers.d: Subcarriers remain orthogonal without a cyclic prefix

explanations.a: Correct! Narrow subcarrier bandwidth ensures flat fading per subcarrier, simplifying equalization.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Each subcarrier may experience a different channel gain.
explanations.c: Incorrect. OFDM uses cyclic prefix, not time-domain filtering, to manage multipath.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Cyclic prefix is essential to preserve orthogonality in multipath channels.

questions.question: What is the effect of increasing the number of OFDM subcarriers while keeping bandwidth fixed?

answers.a: Decreases symbol duration and increases ICI
answers.b: Reduces subcarrier spacing and increases sensitivity to Doppler
answers.c: Increases total bandwidth and reduces SNR

explanations.a: Incorrect. Symbol duration actually increases with more subcarriers.
explanations.b: Correct! Smaller subcarrier spacing improves frequency resolution but makes the system more Doppler-sensitive.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Total bandwidth remains fixed; SNR is not directly affected.
explanations.d: Incorrect. More subcarriers increase complexity in channel estimation.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the cyclic prefix in OFDM under dispersive channels?

answers.a: Eliminates the need for channel equalization
answers.b: Converts linear convolution to circular convolution, preserving orthogonality

explanations.a: Incorrect. Equalization is still needed for channel fading.
explanations.b: Correct! The cyclic prefix allows circular convolution, aligning with FFT processing and avoiding ISI/ICI.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Modulation order is independent of cyclic prefix.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Channel response determines amplitude, not CP.

questions.question: What is a major limitation of OFDM in mobile environments?

answers.b: Time variation within a symbol breaks orthogonality, causing ICI
answers.c: Cannot operate at high data rates

explanations.a: Incorrect. OFDM is sensitive to Doppler.
explanations.b: Correct! Rapid channel changes during a symbol introduce inter-carrier interference.
explanations.c: Incorrect. OFDM supports high data rates efficiently.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Power efficiency depends on amplifier design and PAPR management.

questions.question: In OFDM, what is the effect of increasing modulation order?

answers.a: Improves noise immunity but reduces data rate
answers.b: Increases data rate but also increases BER in low-SNR channels
answers.c: Reduces PAPR due to simpler constellations
answers.d: Eliminates the need for cyclic prefix

explanations.a: Incorrect. Higher-order modulation is less robust to noise.
explanations.b: Correct! Higher-order Modulation increases throughput but is more error-prone under noise.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Higher-order Modulation often increases PAPR.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Cyclic prefix is needed regardless of modulation.

questions.question: Why is IFFT used at the OFDM transmitter?

answers.a: To convert time-domain data to frequency domain
answers.b: To spread the data using coding
answers.c: To generate orthogonal time-domain waveform from frequency-domain symbols

explanations.a: Incorrect. IFFT maps frequency-domain symbols to time domain.
explanations.b: Incorrect. Coding is separate from IFFT.
explanations.c: Correct! IFFT produces a composite orthogonal time-domain signal from frequency-domain data.
explanations.d: Incorrect. IFFT does not perform filtering.

questions.question: In an OFDM system, how does channel estimation error primarily affect system performance?

answers.b: It leads to incorrect equalization, increasing symbol detection errors

explanations.a: Incorrect. Cyclic prefix overhead is independent of channel estimation.
explanations.b: Correct! Inaccurate channel estimates result in improper equalization, leading to higher BER.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Subcarrier spacing is fixed by system design.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Channel estimation errors worsen ISI/ICI effects rather than eliminate them.

questions.question: How does adaptive modulation improve spectral efficiency in OFDM?

answers.a: By assigning modulation order per subcarrier according to channel conditions
answers.b: By varying cyclic prefix length across symbols
answers.c: By transmitting pilot symbols on all subcarriers
answers.d: By reducing IFFT size to fit more data per symbol

explanations.a: Correct! Higher-order modulation is used on good channels and lower-order on poor channels, optimizing throughput.
explanations.b: Incorrect. CP length is generally fixed.
explanations.c: Incorrect. Pilots assist channel estimation, not spectral efficiency.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Reducing IFFT size reduces subcarrier count, not efficiency.

questions.question: How does timing offset at the OFDM receiver affect performance?

answers.b: Breaks subcarrier orthogonality, causing inter-carrier interference

explanations.a: Incorrect. Timing offset does not change frequency directly.
explanations.b: Correct! Timing errors misalign the FFT window, causing Inter-Carrier Interference (ICI).
explanations.c: Incorrect. Amplitude is unaffected by timing offset.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Timing errors degrade synchronization, not improve it.

questions.question: In an OFDM system, what is the primary consequence of a channel delay spread exceeding the cyclic prefix duration?

answers.b: Loss of orthogonality causing both ISI and inter-carrier interference

explanations.a: Incorrect. Performance degrades rather than improves.
explanations.b: Correct! When delay spread exceeds CP, linear convolution is no longer fully converted to circular convolution, leading to ISI and ICI.
explanations.c: Incorrect. PAPR is unrelated to delay spread.
explanations.d: Incorrect. Subcarrier spacing is fixed and not affected by channel delay spread.
